authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Mad River Reach A (mouth to 200 yards upstream)
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth at the Pacific Ocean, near McKinleyville

reaches.name: Mad River Reach B (200 yards above mouth to confluence with Cowan Creek)
Mad River Reach C (Cowan Creek to Deer Creek)
Mad River Reach D (Deer Creek to Ruth Dam)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(60) — Mad River and tributaries (Humboldt Co.)
sources.quotedText: (60) Mad River and tributaries (Humboldt Co.). (A) Mad River from the mouth to 200 yards upstream. Jan. 1 through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) Mad River main stem, from 200 yards above its mouth upstream to the confluence with Cowan Creek, excluding tributaries. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (C) Mad River main stem, from the confluence with Cowan Creek to the confluence with Deer Creek, excluding tributaries. Closed to all fishing all year. (D) Mad River main stem from the confluence with Deer Creek to Ruth Dam. Fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — footnote definition of hatchery trout/steelhead (**)
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
Mad River — California Northcoast Regional Water Quality Control Board
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Open January 1 through March 31 from the mouth to 200 yards upstream.
regulations.verbatimText: Mad River from the mouth to 200 yards upstream. Jan. 1 through Mar. 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily; 4 in possession, at the river mouth reach.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Season spans across the calendar year: opens the fourth Saturday in May and runs through March 31 of the following year.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the fourth Saturday in May through March 31, from 200 yards above the mouth to the confluence with Cowan Creek (excluding tributaries).
regulations.verbatimText: Mad River main stem, from 200 yards above its mouth upstream to the confluence with Cowan Creek, excluding tributaries. Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31; only barbless hooks (bait permitted) may be used from November 1 through March 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks required late May through October; barbless hooks (bait allowed) required November through March.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily; 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing all year from the confluence with Cowan Creek to the confluence with Deer Creek, excluding tributaries.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing year-round, from Cowan Creek confluence to Deer Creek confluence.
regulations.verbatimText: Mad River main stem, from the confluence with Cowan Creek to the confluence with Deer Creek, excluding tributaries. Closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the fourth Saturday in May through October 31, from the Deer Creek confluence to Ruth Dam.
regulations.verbatimText: Mad River main stem from the confluence with Deer Creek to Ruth Dam. Fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily; 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only hatchery trout or steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained; wild (unclipped) trout or steelhead must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released immediately; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish count toward the bag.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
